title: gonocarpus hexander subsp. integrifolius
intro: Gonocarpus hexander subsp. integrifolius, also known as the Australian forked pondweed or simply forked pondweed, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Haloragaceae family. It is native to Australia and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin conditions, with its extracts also showing potential in reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves of Gonocarpus hexander subsp. integrifolius can be dried and made into tea or infused in oil to create topical treatments for various health benefits.
